source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane/Chain Fall Incident

context.region: Gulf of America - Green Canyon Block 868

event.top_event: Unsecured pipe section rolled off storage rack and struck personnel
actions_taken: Injured personnel escorted to rig hospital
Medivac helicopter evacuation to University Hospital New Orleans
TOI investigation team assembled and investigation initiated
event.summary: On 08-MAY-2025 at 0430 hours, two Transocean employees (2nd Engineer and 3rd Engineer/IP) were transferring nine sections of Hawse piping from deck to a three-tier storage rack using chain falls on the Deepwater Invictus drillship. After placing the eighth pipe section on the top tier, the 2E left to retrieve a U-bolt for securing the pipe while the IP knelt to rig the next section. The unsecured eighth pipe section rolled off the rack, fell approximately 3 feet, and struck the IP's right middle finger, resulting in amputation of the fingertip at the DIP joint. The IP was evacuated by medivac helicopter and treated at University Hospital. Root causes identified include flawed rack design, inadequate hazard analysis in the HIPC, lack of written job procedure, and failure to secure pipe before removing chain falls.
recommendations: BSEE Office of Safety Management to evaluate the Hazard ID Prompt (HIPC) card used by Transocean
Install additional restraints on storage rack such as chalks, bumpers, or grooves
Implement written job procedure for pipe storage task
Enhance hazard identification to specifically identify unsecured pipe rolling hazard and crush points
Establish requirement to secure piping with U-bolts before removing chain falls

hazards.name: Unsecured heavy pipe on elevated storage rack
Flawed storage rack design without adequate restraints
Personnel working in proximity to suspended or stored loads

threats.name: Pipe section rolling off storage rack due to inadequate securing
Personnel positioned in fall path of unsecured load
Failure to identify hazards during pre-job briefing

consequences.name: Restricted duty status for injured worker

controls.name: Pre-job briefing using Hazard ID Prompt Card (HIPC)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and assign responsibilities before task execution
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to prevent hazard exposure

supporting_text: The HIPC did identify pinch points as a hazard, the HIPC did not specify where the hazard existed
The HIPC failed to recognize unsecured pipe rolling off the rack creating a potential crush point
The HIPC did not identify chocking or securing the piping before removing the chain falls
BSEE found it to be inadequate

controls.name: Securing pipe with U-bolts before removing chain falls
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unsecured pipe from rolling off rack
controls.lod_basis: Physical restraint to prevent load movement

supporting_text: After placing the 8th section of pipe on the third (uppermost) tier of the storage rack, the 2E and IP removed the chain falls from the pipe
The largest factor in this incident was the failure to secure the pipe before removing the chain falls
The rack was designed to secure the sections of pipe to the rack with U-bolts

controls.name: Storage rack design with adequate restraint capacity
controls.barrier_role: Provide structural support and restraint for stored pipes
controls.lod_basis: Engineering design to prevent load movement

supporting_text: The design of the rack may not be suitable for pipe support without additional restraints
The rack was designed to secure the sections of pipe to the rack with U-bolts
TOI report identified improvements like chalks, bumpers, grooves, etc. to be installed on the supports and increasing the angle of the supports

controls.name: Written job procedure for pipe storage task
controls.barrier_role: Provide documented work instructions and safety requirements
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to standardize work execution

supporting_text: It was also noted there was no written job procedure for the task being performed

controls.name: Proper PPE (Personal Protective Equipment)
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of injury from crush hazard
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: A positive from the HIPC was that proper PPE was to be worn
The IP's witness statement confirmed that he was wearing the proper PPE

controls.name: Safe lifting practices identification in HIPC
controls.barrier_role: Establish safe work practices for lifting operations
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to guide safe execution

supporting_text: The HIPC identified safe lifting practices as part of the plan
It did not identify chocking or securing the piping before removing the chain falls
Neither 2E nor the IP were aware of the immediate danger

controls.name: Hazard awareness and situational awareness during task execution
controls.barrier_role: Detect and avoid hazardous conditions during work

supporting_text: 2E and the IP failed to observe the hazard of unsecured pipes on the rack
Neither 2E nor the IP were aware of the immediate danger

Another immediate cause identified in the report was that IP placed himself in danger when he knelt on the deck to rig up the next section of Hawse pipe before the previously lifted section of pipe had been secured

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
